name: Transformations — Slide, Flip, Turn and Resize
whatIsIt: A TRANSFORMATION moves or resizes a shape. There are four kinds: TRANSLATION (slide the shape without turning), REFLECTION (flip it over a mirror line), ROTATION (turn it about a point by an angle), and ENLARGEMENT (scale it bigger or smaller by a factor). In a translation, reflection and rotation the shape keeps its SIZE (congruent). In an enlargement the shape keeps its SHAPE but changes size (similar).
whyNeeded: Transformations describe how patterns, tiles, logos, animations and mirror images are built. They explain symmetry, why a reflection swaps left and right, and are a core topic in ICSE, IGCSE and international geometry — appearing in almost every exam.
realLifeUses: Sliding a chess piece across the board (translation)
Your mirror image in a mirror (reflection)
The hands of a clock turning (rotation)
Zooming a photo bigger on a phone (enlargement)

vocabulary.meaning: A slide — every point moves the same distance and direction, no turning.
A flip across a mirror line; the image is the same distance behind the line.
A turn about a fixed point by a given angle and direction.
A resize by a scale factor from a centre; shape stays the same, size changes.

story.title: The Rangoli Pattern
story.text: Meera draws one petal for her Diwali rangoli. Nani says, 'Now don't redraw it — TRANSFORM it!' Meera slides a copy to the right (translation), flips one across a line to face the other way (reflection), and turns copies around the centre point (rotation) to make a full flower. Then she makes a giant version for the doorway by scaling every length ×3 (enlargement). 'Four moves,' says Nani, 'and one petal became a whole rangoli.'
story.extractedProblem: Meera turns a petal around the centre so 4 copies fill the circle equally. Through what angle is each copy rotated?
story.answerInStory: 90° each — a full turn of 360° shared by 4 copies is 360 ÷ 4 = 90°.

visual.caption: A translation slides the shape to a new place without turning or resizing it — the image is congruent to the original.

teachingGallery.title: The four transformations
teachingGallery.note: Slide, flip, turn keep the size; enlarge changes it.

examples.caption: Reflection: flipped across a mirror line.

standardMethod.summary: Name the transformation, then apply its rule. Translation: shift every point by the same vector. Reflection: each point goes the same distance to the other side of the mirror line. Rotation: turn every point about the centre by the angle. Enlargement: multiply each distance from the centre by the scale factor.
steps: Decide which of the four transformations is happening (slide, flip, turn, resize).
For a translation, move every corner the same right/left and up/down amount.
For a reflection, place each point the same distance on the opposite side of the mirror line.
For a rotation, turn each point about the centre by the given angle; for an enlargement, multiply each length from the centre by the scale factor.

workedExamples.problem: A point at (2, 3) is translated 4 right and 1 down. Where does it land?
A shape is enlarged by scale factor 3. A side of 5 cm becomes how long?
steps: Enlargement multiplies each length by the factor.

commonMistakes.mistake: Thinking a slide, flip or turn changes the size of the shape.
commonMistakes.fix: Translation, reflection and rotation keep the SIZE — the image is congruent. Only an ENLARGEMENT changes size.

commonMistakes.mistake: Drawing the reflection on the same side as the shape.
commonMistakes.fix: A reflection is on the OPPOSITE side of the mirror line, the SAME distance away as the original point.

commonMistakes.mistake: Naming the wrong transformation for a move (e.g. calling a flip an enlargement).
commonMistakes.fix: Match the move: slide = translation, flip = reflection, turn = rotation, resize = enlargement.

easy.q: Sliding a shape without turning it is a…
Your image in a flat mirror is a…

medium.q: Which transformation CHANGES the size of a shape?
A shape turns fully (360°) into 4 equal copies around a point. Each rotation is how many degrees?

challenge.q: Point (1, 5) is translated 3 right and 2 down. Its new y-coordinate?
Enlargement scale factor 4. A 6 cm side becomes (cm)?

questions.q: A flip across a mirror line is a…
A turn about a point is a…
Which keeps the shape's size the SAME?
Enlargement scale factor 5; a 3 cm side becomes (cm)?

teachBackPrompt: Cut out a paper triangle. Show a grown-up all four transformations: slide it (translation), flip it over a pencil line (reflection), turn it around a pin (rotation), and describe an enlargement ×2. Explain which three keep the size the same and which one changes it.

revisionCard.summary: Four transformations: TRANSLATION (slide), REFLECTION (flip over a mirror line), ROTATION (turn about a point), ENLARGEMENT (resize by a scale factor). Slide/flip/turn keep size (congruent); enlargement keeps shape but changes size (similar).

rememberIt.hook: Slide, Flip, Turn, Resize = Translate, Reflect, Rotate, Enlarge
rememberIt.unpack: First three keep size (congruent); enlargement changes size (similar).

studentQuestions.q: Which transformations keep the shape congruent?
studentQuestions.a: Translation, reflection and rotation — they move the shape but never change its size or shape.

studentQuestions.q: What does a negative scale factor do in an enlargement?
studentQuestions.a: It enlarges AND flips the shape to the other side of the centre point (an upside-down, resized image).
